description: Roadmap from v0.1.0-alpha to v1.0: close correctness gaps (cross-file inheritance, type inference, scope leaks), add LSP capabilities (semantic tokens, document highlights, folding, signature help, code actions, workspace symbol), and harden infrastructure (incremental rebuilds, background indexing, persistent cache, config UI).

userStories.title: Cross-file inheritance lookup in wireInheritance
userStories.description: As a developer, I need wireInheritance() to look up inherited classes via WorkspaceIndex when not found in the same file, so that Dog d; d->speak() resolves speak() when Animal is in a different file.
acceptanceCriteria: wireInheritance() calls WorkspaceIndex to find classes not present in the current file's symbol table
When Animal is in file_b.pike and Dog inherits Animal in file_a.pike, Dog's inheritedScopes includes Animal's scope
Existing same-file inheritance tests still pass (backward compatible)
Add at least 3 tests: cross-file inherit completion, cross-file inherit definition, cross-file inherit hover
Tests use existing corpus files (cross_inherit_chain.pike, class-single-inherit.pike)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key file: server/src/features/workspaceIndex.ts (wireInheritance method). Depends on existing WorkspaceIndex.getFile() and ModuleResolver. See known limitation in docs/known-limitations.md section 'Cross-file inherited member completion not supported'.

userStories.title: Cross-file inherited member completion
userStories.description: As a user, I want Dog d; d-> to show inherited members like speak() from Animal, even when Animal is defined in another file.
acceptanceCriteria: Completion after d-> includes members from cross-file inherited classes
No duplicate entries when member exists in both same-file and inherited class
Add test: Dog in file_a inherits Animal in file_b, d-> returns speak() from Animal
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Key file: server/src/features/completion.ts (resolveMemberAccess usage). After US-001 wires cross-file inheritance, completion should automatically pick up inherited members.

userStories.title: Cross-file inherited member definition and hover
userStories.description: As a user, I want go-to-definition on Dog d; d->speak() to jump to Animal.speak() in the other file, and hover to show Animal's speak documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Go-to-definition on d->speak() jumps to the file containing Animal and highlights speak()
Hover on d->speak() shows the signature/documentation from Animal.speak()
Add test for cross-file inherited definition resolution
Add test for cross-file inherited hover resolution
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Key files: server/src/features/accessResolver.ts (resolveAccessDefinition, resolveAccessDeclaration), server/src/server.ts (onDefinition, onHover handlers).

userStories.title: Cross-file inherited member rename scope precision
userStories.description: As a user, I want renaming bark() on Dog to NOT rename bark() on Cat when they share the same method name.
acceptanceCriteria: When renaming bark() on Dog, arrow/dot access references are filtered by resolved type where type is known
If type is unresolved, keep current name-based matching (no behavior change for unresolvable cases)
Add test: two classes with same method name, rename one does not affect the other
Existing rename tests still pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key file: server/src/features/rename.ts (getRenameLocations). The arrow/dot access references currently use name-based matching. After US-001 wires cross-file types, use resolveMemberAccess to check if the reference's type resolves to the target class.

userStories.title: Scope handlers for while, switch, and do-while blocks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-construct scope handlers for while, switch, and do-while blocks so variables declared inside don't leak to the enclosing scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Add collectWhileStatement() in symbolTable.ts that creates a block scope for while_statement body
Add collectDoWhileStatement() in symbolTable.ts that creates a block scope for do_while_statement body
Add collectSwitchStatement() in symbolTable.ts that creates a block scope for switch_statement body
Variables declared inside while/switch/do-while blocks are scoped to that block
All existing 1,311+ tests pass (backward compatible)
Add 3+ tests: one per construct, verifying variables don't leak
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key file: server/src/features/symbolTable.ts. Follow the pattern of collectIfStatement() and collectForStatement(). Upstream issue: tree-sitter-pike#4. These are workarounds since tree-sitter doesn't provide scope-introducing nodes for these constructs.

userStories.title: Full scope chain for local variable hover
userStories.description: As a user, I want hover on any local variable in a complex function, not just ones in the immediate scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Hover resolves variables declared in outer scopes of deeply nested functions
Add corpus file with deeply nested function (3+ nesting levels) to exercise scope chain walking
Add tests verifying hover on variables at each nesting level
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts (onHover), server/src/features/symbolTable.ts (getDefinitionAt). The scope chain walk in getDefinitionAt may be stopping early for deeply nested scopes. Investigate whether the issue is in scope chain construction or in the walk itself.

userStories.title: Function return type tracking in type resolver
userStories.description: As a user, I want makeDog()->speak() to resolve speak() because makeDog() has return type Dog.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveType() resolves through function return type annotations (e.g., Dog makeDog() returns Dog)
resolveMemberAccess() uses resolved return type for chained access
makeDog()->speak() completion returns speak() when makeDog has return type Dog
makeDog()->speak() go-to-definition jumps to Dog.speak()
Depth-limited to MAX_RESOLUTION_DEPTH (already 5) to prevent infinite recursion
Add 5+ tests: return type inference, chained calls, depth limit, failure cases
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key file: server/src/features/typeResolver.ts. resolveType() currently only resolves declared types on variables/parameters. Extend it to also look up function declarations by name, check their return type, and resolve that. Decision 0014 describes the existing architecture.

userStories.title: Assignment-based type narrowing in symbol table
userStories.description: As a user, I want Dog d = makeDog(); d->speak() to resolve even without an explicit type annotation on d, because the assignment RHS is Dog.
acceptanceCriteria: Declaration interface gains optional assignedType field
Symbol table builder records initializer type for variable declarations when RHS is a simple type reference
resolveMemberAccess() checks assignedType when declaredType is absent or mixed
Only track simple assignments (x = TypeName), not control-flow-sensitive narrowing
Add 4+ tests: assignment inference, mixed variable with assignment, no initializer, complex initializer (ignored)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007. Key files: server/src/features/symbolTable.ts (Declaration type, buildSymbolTable), server/src/features/typeResolver.ts. The initializer is available in tree-sitter's local_declaration node (the RHS of the assignment). Only resolve if the RHS is a single identifier that resolves to a class.

userStories.title: PikeWorker typeof integration for hover on mixed variables
userStories.description: As a user, I want hover on mixed x = someFunction() to show the inferred type from Pike, not just mixed.
acceptanceCriteria: Hover on local variable with declaredType mixed or absent queries PikeWorker typeof()
Falls back to declared/assigned type if worker unavailable or times out (no added latency for typed vars)
Explicitly typed variables (int x) skip typeof() call entirely
Add 3+ tests: mixed variable hover with typeof, typed variable skips typeof, worker timeout fallback
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts (declForHover), server/src/features/pikeWorker.ts. PikeWorker already has a typeof method wired. The integration point is in declForHover() — after Tier 3 (tree-sitter) returns mixed, make an async call to worker.typeof() and use the result if available.

userStories.title: Type inference corpus files and harness snapshots
userStories.description: As a developer, I need corpus files that exercise each inference path with ground-truth snapshots from Pike.
acceptanceCriteria: Add corpus file for return type inference: function returning typed object, caller accessing members
Add corpus file for assignment inference: variable assigned from typed function, accessing members
Add corpus file for chained inference: a()->b()->c pattern
Add corpus file for inference failure: mixed returns, unknown types
Harness snapshots generated for each new corpus file
Existing harness tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: corpus/files/ (new .pike files), harness/snapshots/ (new JSON). Follow existing corpus file patterns. Run bun run harness:verify after adding files.

userStories.title: Decision document for type inference and Phase 10 audit
userStories.description: As a developer, I need decision documents for the type inference architecture and an audit of Decision 0002 to confirm type resolution boundaries are still accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Write decisions/0019-type-inference.md covering: return type tracking, assignment narrowing, typeof integration, depth limits, fallback behavior
Update decisions/0002-tier-3-scope.md: reassess undocumented object members gap after Phase 10/11 improvements
Update docs/known-limitations.md: mark resolved items, document remaining gaps
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a documentation-only story. No code changes. Follow the ADR template in decisions/0000-template.md.

userStories.title: Semantic token type and modifier mapping
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a defined mapping from Pike's declaration types to LSP SemanticTokenTypes and SemanticTokenModifiers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/src/features/semanticTokens.ts with the token type mapping: class->class, function->function, method->method, variable->variable, parameter->parameter, constant->variable(modifier:readonly), enum->enum, enum_member->enumMember, typedef->type, inherit/import->namespace
Define modifier mapping: declaration, definition, readonly (constants), static (class-level declarations), deprecated (from AutoDoc)
Token type and modifier indices follow LSP spec (legend ordering)
Write decisions/0020-semantic-tokens.md with the mapping rationale
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a data model and design story. No LSP handler yet. Key reference: LSP spec 3.16+ semantic tokens. Key file: server/src/features/symbolTable.ts (DeclKind, Declaration types). Each DeclKind maps to a SemanticTokenType.

userStories.title: Semantic token production from symbol table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that walks the symbol table and tree-sitter parse tree to produce semantic token data (delta-encoded array per LSP spec).
acceptanceCriteria: Add produceSemanticTokens(table: SymbolTable, tree: Tree): SemanticToken[] function
Walks all declarations in the symbol table, producing one token per declaration with correct type and modifiers
Handles references: identifier_expr, arrow_access, dot_access, scope_access get appropriate token types
Stdlib/predef identifiers tokenized as namespace or type (not variable)
Tokens are delta-encoded (relative positions) per LSP spec
Add 10+ unit tests for the token production function
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/features/semanticTokens.ts (new), server/src/features/symbolTable.ts (SymbolTable, Declaration, Reference types). This story produces the data, NOT the LSP handler.

userStories.title: Semantic token LSP handler registration
userStories.description: As a user, I want my Pike code to be semantically highlighted with correct token types for classes, functions, variables, parameters, etc.
acceptanceCriteria: Register textDocument/semanticTokens/full handler in server.ts
Register semanticTokens capability in initialize response with full delta support
Handler calls produceSemanticTokens() from US-013 and returns the LSP-encoded response
Incremental token updates on didChange (delta encoding per LSP spec)
Add 5+ LSP protocol tests (Layer 1) verifying token output for various declaration types
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-012 and US-013. Key file: server/src/server.ts (add onSemanticTokens handler). Follow the pattern of onDocumentSymbol — get doc, parse, produce tokens, return.

userStories.title: Document highlight handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all occurrences of the symbol under my cursor highlighted in the current file.
acceptanceCriteria: Register textDocument/documentHighlight handler in server.ts
Uses getReferencesTo() from symbolTable (same-file, already implemented)
Distinguishes read (DocumentHighlightKind.Read) vs write (DocumentHighlightKind.Write) access
Register documentHighlightProvider capability in initialize response
Add 5+ tests: variable read/write, function call, class usage, parameter, enum member
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts, server/src/features/symbolTable.ts (getReferencesTo). getReferencesTo already returns Reference objects with location data. Map them to DocumentHighlight with Read/Write kind based on the reference context.

userStories.title: Folding range handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want to fold class bodies, function bodies, if/for/foreach/while/switch blocks, import groups, and comment blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: Register textDocument/foldingRange handler in server.ts
Register foldingRangeProvider capability in initialize response
Foldable ranges from tree-sitter: class_body, function_body, if/for/foreach/while/switch blocks
Foldable ranges for comment blocks (line_comment groups) and AutoDoc blocks (//! groups)
Add 6+ tests: one per foldable construct type
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts, server/src/parser.ts (tree-sitter parse). This uses the tree-sitter parse tree directly — walk the tree for nodes that have block-like children and produce FoldingRange objects.

userStories.title: Signature help handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want parameter hints when calling a function or method, showing which parameter I am currently typing.
acceptanceCriteria: Register textDocument/signatureHelp handler in server.ts
Trigger characters: ( and ,
Register signatureHelpProvider capability with trigger characters
Shows parameter name and type from tree-sitter declaration for local/workspace functions
Shows signature from stdlib-autodoc.json for stdlib functions
Active parameter tracking based on comma count inside the argument list
Write decisions/0021-signature-help.md
Add 5+ tests: local function, class method, stdlib function, nested calls, no match
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts, server/src/features/symbolTable.ts, server/src/data/stdlib-autodoc.json. Use tree-sitter to find the enclosing call expression, identify the callee, resolve it to a declaration, extract parameter list.

userStories.title: Code action: remove unused variable
userStories.description: As a user, I want a quick-fix to remove unused variables when the Pike compiler reports them.
acceptanceCriteria: Register textDocument/codeAction handler in server.ts
Register codeActionProvider capability in initialize response
Code action: 'Remove unused variable' appears for diagnostics with message matching 'Unused local variable'
Action produces a WorkspaceEdit that deletes the declaration line
Add 3+ tests: unused variable gets action, used variable gets no action, action produces correct edit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts, server/src/features/diagnosticManager.ts (diagnostics source). This is the simplest code action — start here before adding more complex ones.

userStories.title: Code action: add missing import
userStories.description: As a user, I want a quick-fix to add an import when the Pike compiler reports an undefined identifier that matches a known module.
acceptanceCriteria: Code action: 'Add import X' appears for undefined identifier diagnostics where X matches a known module name
Module matching uses WorkspaceIndex module map and stdlib index
Action produces a WorkspaceEdit inserting import statement at file top (after any existing imports or #pike directives)
Add 3+ tests: undefined identifier matching stdlib module, matching workspace module, no match gets no action
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-018 (code action handler already registered). Key files: server/src/server.ts, server/src/features/workspaceIndex.ts (module map), server/src/data/stdlib-autodoc.json.

userStories.title: Workspace symbol search handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search for symbols across the entire workspace (Ctrl+T / workspace/symbol).
acceptanceCriteria: Register workspace/symbol handler in server.ts
Searches across all indexed files in WorkspaceIndex
Supports prefix matching on symbol names (case-insensitive)
Returns SymbolInformation with name, kind, location (URI + range)
Lazy: only searches files that have been indexed (opened or changed)
Register workspace.symbolProvider capability in initialize response
Add 4+ tests: search by class name, function name, partial match, no results
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts, server/src/features/workspaceIndex.ts. Iterate over all FileEntry objects in the index, collect declarations whose name matches the query, map to SymbolInformation.

userStories.title: Background workspace indexing on startup
userStories.description: As a user, I want the LSP to index my entire workspace on startup so that workspace/symbol and cross-file navigation work immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: On initialized event, kick off background file discovery of workspace .pike/.pmod files
Parse and index each discovered file into WorkspaceIndex
Report progress via window/workDoneProgress
Does not block or degrade responsiveness of active editing (async, yields between files)
Add 3+ tests: background indexing populates index, progress reported, concurrent requests still respond
Write decisions/0022-background-indexing.md
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts (onInitialized), server/src/features/workspaceIndex.ts. File discovery: use glob pattern **/*.pike and **/*.pmod relative to workspace root. Parse each file with tree-sitter, build symbol table, upsert into index.

userStories.title: Persistent cache across LSP restarts
userStories.description: As a user, I want the LSP to start fast when reopening a workspace because it reuses cached analysis from the previous session.
acceptanceCriteria: On shutdown, serialize symbol tables and workspace index metadata to .pike-lsp/cache/ in the workspace
On startup, load cache and rebuild only files whose content hash changed
Cache invalidated when pike version changes (read pike --version) or tree-sitter-pike WASM hash changes
Graceful fallback: if cache is corrupt, delete and rebuild from scratch (no crash)
Add 4+ tests: cache roundtrip, cache invalidation on version change, corrupt cache recovery, partial rebuild
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: server/src/server.ts (onShutdown, onInitialized), server/src/features/workspaceIndex.ts. Cache format: JSON files keyed by URI. Each entry: URI, version, contentHash, symbolTable (serialized declarations/scopes/references).

userStories.title: VSCode configuration schema and settings UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a VSCode settings UI for the LSP configuration options, not just raw initializationOptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add configuration.contributes.configuration to extension.package.json with schema for: pike.diagnostics.mode (enum: realtime/saveOnly/off), pike.diagnostics.debounceMs (number), pike.diagnostics.maxProblems (number), pike.binaryPath (string)
Add didChangeConfiguration handler in server.ts that reads updated settings
Settings changes take effect without restart
Default values documented in the schema
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: extension.package.json (contributes.configuration), server/src/server.ts (onDidChangeConfiguration handler). The server already reads initializationOptions; extend to also read workspace/configuration.

userStories.title: Cancellation token propagation to all handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all request handlers to respect CancellationToken so stale requests don't compete with new ones.
acceptanceCriteria: onDefinition handler checks token.isCancellationRequested before and after expensive operations
onReferences handler checks token.isCancellationRequested
onHover handler checks token.isCancellationRequested
onRenameRequest handler checks token.isCancellationRequested
onSemanticTokens handler checks token.isCancellationRequested (if implemented)
Return null or empty result immediately when cancelled
Add 4+ tests: cancellation during definition, references, hover, rename
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key file: server/src/server.ts. The onCompletion handler already checks CancellationToken (Phase 6). Extend the pattern to all other handlers. Each handler's signature already includes CancellationToken — just add the checks.

userStories.title: Update Decision 0002 and project documentation for v1.0
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to revise Decision 0002 to include code actions and update all project documentation to reflect the v1.0 feature set.
acceptanceCriteria: Decision 0002 section 13 (code actions) updated from 'out of scope' to 'in scope, implemented'
docs/state-of-project.md updated with Phase 10-14 completions and new capability table
README.md updated: feature list includes all 12 LSP capabilities
docs/other-editors.md verified current for Neovim and generic LSP clients
CHANGELOG.md updated with all changes since alpha
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Documentation-only story. No code changes. Read the existing docs before updating to ensure accuracy.

userStories.title: Real-codebase verification against Pike stdlib
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the LSP against Pike's lib/modules/ (555 files) with all v1.0 features to confirm zero crashes and zero wrong answers.
acceptanceCriteria: Run LSP against Pike's lib/modules/ or equivalent 100+ file Pike codebase
Test documentSymbol, definition, references, hover, completion, rename, semanticTokens, foldingRange, documentHighlight, signatureHelp across 14+ representative files
Zero crashes (P1)
Zero wrong answers (P1) — any P1 found must be fixed before this story passes
All P2 findings documented with specific file, line, feature, expected vs actual
Semantic token coverage measured: % of declarations with correct token type
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the Phase 9 WS2 methodology: select 14 representative files spanning 10-2528 lines, exercise each LSP feature, classify findings as P1 (crash/wrong) or P2 (degraded/missing).

userStories.title: Performance regression test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated performance regression tests so v1.0 stays fast as features are added.
acceptanceCriteria: Add benchmark tests for: completion (cold and warm), hover, definition, semantic tokens, workspace indexing
Benchmarks are separate test file(s) in tests/perf/ marked as slow
CI workflow added that runs benchmarks (separate from main test suite)
Baseline measurements documented in docs/state-of-project.md Performance Baseline section
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key files: tests/perf/ (new directory), .github/workflows/benchmark.yml (new). Use Bun's built-in test runner with performance measurement. Baselines: completion warm p50 <2ms, hover <1ms, semantic tokens full <50ms.

userStories.title: Version bump and v1.0.0 release
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need to tag and release v1.0.0 with tested VSIX and standalone builds.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json version set to 1.0.0
VSIX built via existing build process and tested on clean VSCode installation
Standalone build tested with Neovim + nvim-lspconfig
GitHub release created with changelog from CHANGELOG.md
docs/state-of-project.md updated with v1.0.0 status and final test counts
All tests pass (1,500+ expected)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the final story. All previous stories must pass. Use existing build scripts: bun run build, bun run build:standalone, bun run build:extension.
